function_explanation: Generates a 2D Hamming window by computing the outer product of two 1D Hamming windows and taking the square root of the result.
function_docstring:
Generates a 2D Hamming window of shape (a, b).

    This function constructs a 2D window by taking the outer product of two 1D Hamming
    windows of lengths ``a`` and ``b``, respectively. It then returns the square root
    of this 2D window.

    Args:
        a (int): The length of the first dimension (height) of the window.
        b (int): The length of the second dimension (width) of the window.

    Returns:
        numpy.ndarray: A 2D array of shape (a, b) containing the square root of the
        outer product of the Hamming windows.
usage_example:
import numpy as np
from agent_hamming2d import hamming2d

# Generate a 2D Hamming window of size 10x20
height = 10
width = 20
window = hamming2d(height, width)

print(f"Window shape: {window.shape}")
# Output: Window shape: (10, 20)
print(f"Max value: {np.max(window)}")
